tldr: Mathieu van der Poel took the wet gravel/hilly stage to San Gimignano in a reduced-group sprint, with Isaac del Toro second and Giulio Pellizzari third — same time. Del Toro took the leader's jersey from Ganna's Stage 1 ITT advantage.

narrative.headline: Van der Poel on Italian gravel, Del Toro into the lead
narrative.body: 206 km from Camaiore to San Gimignano in wet conditions; the closing hills produced a reduced front group. Van der Poel won the small-group sprint — a Strade Bianche dress rehearsal five days early.

gcImpact.headline: Del Toro into the jersey
gcImpact.body: Ganna's ITT margin couldn't survive the climbing-and-gravel selection. Del Toro becomes overall leader.

riderPerformances.name: Mathieu van der Poel
riderPerformances.narrative: Won the reduced-bunch sprint after the wet-gravel selection. Set the form line for Strade Bianche, where he'd finish behind Pogačar and Seixas.

sources.title: Van der Poel wins Tirreno Stage 2
